name: Pushkar
description: Pushkar is a magnificent destination in Rajasthan, India. Known for its remarkable blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Visitors flock here to experience its unique atmosphere, vibrant local markets, and iconic landmarks. Whether you are seeking spiritual peace, thrilling adventures, or simply a relaxing holiday, Pushkar offers a memorable experience that captures the true essence of Indian heritage. The city's charm lies in its timeless appeal and warm hospitality.
history: Pushkar boasts a rich history spanning several centuries, serving as a prominent center for trade, culture, and power under various dynasties.

topAttractions.name: Brahma Temple
topAttractions.description: One of the very few temples in the world dedicated to Brahma the Creator (other major Brahma temples are at Khokhan in HP and Asotra in Rajasthan). The 14th-century marble temple with a distinctive red shikara is in the center of Pushkar town. Non-Hindus are permitted. Entry: Free. Strict rules: no leather products, no photography inside.

topAttractions.name: Pushkar Lake (52 Ghats)
topAttractions.description: A sacred lake believed to have formed from a lotus petal dropped by Brahma. 52 bathing ghats (stone steps) line the lake — Brahma Ghat (most sacred), Gau Ghat, VIP Ghat. Bathing is believed to wash away sins. Sunset and sunrise on the ghats are profoundly beautiful. No non-vegetarian food and no alcohol are served anywhere near the lake.

topAttractions.name: Savitri Temple
topAttractions.description: A hilltop temple dedicated to Brahma's wife Savitri — the reason Brahma has few temples (she cursed him for using Gayatri in her place at the Pushkar yajna). The 3km climb up Ratnagiri Hill (1.5 hrs or ropeway ₹150 return) rewards with panoramic views of the lake, town, and Thar Desert. Best at sunset.

topAttractions.name: Pushkar Camel Fair (November)
topAttractions.description: Asia's largest camel fair — held on the Kartik Purnima full moon (November). 200,000+ visitors, 50,000+ camels and livestock traded. Camel races, folk performances, turban-tying contests. The sand dunes fill with colourful tents. Book accommodation 6 months ahead. 5-day event.

topAttractions.name: Rangji Temple
topAttractions.description: A colorful South Indian-style Vaishnava temple (Pushkar has a large South Indian pilgrim community) with a 24m tall gopuram tower — unusual in Rajasthan. Entry: Free. Interesting contrast to the predominantly North Indian temple architecture.

topAttractions.name: Camel, Horse & Desert Safari
topAttractions.description: Pushkar is a base for exploring the Thar Desert fringes. Camel rides around the lake and to the desert dunes (₹300-500/1 hr). Overnight desert camps (15km from Pushkar, ₹2000-5000 with folk music and dinner). Horse safaris are also available through equestrian centers in town.

culture: The culture of Pushkar is a vibrant tapestry of traditional arts, festivals, and culinary delights, deeply influenced by its historical roots.
travelTips: Book accommodations in advance during peak season
Try the local street food but ensure it is hot and fresh
Use authorized taxis or rideshare apps
Respect local customs and dress modestly at religious sites

itineraries.name: Highlights Tour
activities: Day 1: Arrival, City Center Attractions, Evening Market
Day 2: Historical Sites, Local Food Tasting, Departure
